title: identity — outbound webhooks
description: Identity DISPATCHES no outbound webhooks. It is a security substrate — exposing identity lifecycle events to tenant-configurable webhook endpoints would leak PII to third parties. Tenants that need identity-lifecycle callbacks can subscribe via `platform/webhook` to events published by higher-tier modules (`identity/user::UserProvisioned`, `identity/auth::LoginSucceeded`) which are redacted before dispatch.

inbound_webhook_kinds.name: kms.key.rotated
inbound_webhook_kinds.description: Optional AWS EventBridge integration — KMS notifies the app when the key backing MFA-secret encryption rotates. Received event triggers the `ScheduleMfaSecretRotation` listener (see listeners.json) which runs a bulk re-encryption sweep.
inbound_webhook_kinds.notes: Best-effort. Missing the event is not catastrophic — the rotation sweep also runs via `identity:rotate-mfa-secret` cron entry per environment policy.

rationale: PII redaction over a webhook boundary is a hard problem — downstream systems keep webhooks for years and reconstruct identities from correlation. Cleaner posture: don't dispatch. Tenants that legitimately need cross-system identity sync should use SCIM (deferred to Wave 1c) not webhooks.
The `identity/auth` module MAY dispatch a redacted `AuthEventEmitted` webhook for tenants that opt in — that's an auth-flow concern, not an identity-substrate concern.
